title: Monmouth Beach: Sand, Scandal, and the Shore | New Jersey Documentary
description:
The untold story of Monmouth Beach, New Jersey -- from Lenape settlements to Gilded Age scandal, heroic life-saving stations, Prohibition rum-runners, and Hurricane Sandy's devastation.

This documentary traces 12,000 years of history on a narrow strip of sand between the Atlantic Ocean and the Shrewsbury River. Featuring historical maps from the 1873 Beers Atlas, 1879 NOAA Coast Survey, Rutgers University, and Library of Congress, plus personal photography from Hurricane Sandy's aftermath.

Chapters:
0:00 - The False Lights Legend
0:20 - The First People (12,000 Years Ago)
1:00 - Land Pirates & Wreckers
1:50 - The Storm Warriors (U.S. Life-Saving Service)
2:40 - Millionaire's Row (Ocean Avenue)
3:40 - The Scandal (George M. Robeson)
4:20 - The Superbanker (George Fisher Baker)
4:50 - Galilee & The Fishermen
5:25 - Rum Runners (Prohibition)
6:05 - The Ocean Always Wins (Hurricane Sandy)
6:40 - Endurance

---

Directed by Vince Verite
Written by Stella Storycraft
Visual Assets by Artie Aesthetic
Assembled by Franco Framerate

Photography by Nick DeMarco
Produced by Nick DeMarco
